text: Tom has some red marbles and some blue marbles. He has 20 marbles in total. He has 4 more red marbles than blue marbles. How many blue marbles does Tom have?
generatorSolution: Let the number of blue marbles = b. Then the number of red marbles = b + 4. Together they make 20, so: b + (b + 4) = 20. This gives 2b + 4 = 20. Subtract 4 from both sides: 2b = 16. Divide both sides by 2: b = 8. So Tom has 8 blue marbles.
